docs.title: Spello Consulting Smart Device Libary
docs.text:
The SCSmartDevice package is designed to make it easy to integrate with Shelly and Tasmota smart switches. Specifically this library supports switches and energy meter as allows you to:

  • Get the status of a device including temperature, Mac address, etc.
  • See if a device is online or not
  • Read the current state of an input switch
  • Read the current state of an output relay
  • Read an energy meter including power, energy, voltage, current and power factor (where available)
  • Get the temperature from one or more digitial probes (connected to a Shelly Add-on)
  • Change the state of an output relay (i.e. turn on or off)
  • Respond to webhooks generated by the Shelly device, so that your app can wake immediately when an event occurs, such as an input being turned on.

The package also implements a thread safe, read only cache of the device objects (the SmartDeviceView class) and a SmartDeviceWorker thread that allows your application to asynchroniously interface with the hardware.

docs.title: Shelly Devices
docs.text:
This package supports many device types (30 at the last count) including older models no longer sold by the vendors.

This package also isolates you from having to deal with the idiosyncrasies of the Shelly APIs. The vendor completely changed the API for generation 2 devices and later, and even with the new API it’s rather complex deal with the different model types and the API calls they support. This package includes a shelly_models.json file that defines the capabilities of each model. Use the print_model_library() method to view this information.

docs.title: Tasmota Devices
docs.text: This package supports Tasmota smart switches (ESP32 hardware, tasmota32 v13 API). Currently this package only supports switches (including e-meters and internal temperature probes). At a later date support will be added for periphals and matter event signaling.

docs.title: Installing the package
docs.text:
The package is available from PyPi, so to add it to your Python project use pip:

pip install sc-smart-switch

Or better yet, use UV:

uv add sc-smart-switch

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text: The library uses a YAML file for configuration. An example config file (examples/switch_config.yaml) is included in this package. See the example config page.

docs.title: Example Configuration File
docs.text:
Files:
    LogfileName: examples/logfile.log
    LogfileVerbosity: detailed
    ConsoleVerbosity: detailed


  SCSmartDevices:
    AllowDebugLogging: False
    ResponseTimeout: 3
    RetryCount: 1
    RetryDelay: 2
    PingAllowed: True
    SimulationFileFolder: simulation_files
    ShellyWebhooks:
      Enabled: True
      Host: 192.168.86.47
      Port: 8787
      Path: /shelly/webhook
      DefaultWebhooks:
        Inputs:
          - input.toggle_on
          - input.toggle_off
          - input.button_push
        Outputs:
          - switch.on
          - switch.off  
    Devices:
      - Name: Sydney Dev A
        Model: Shelly2PMG3
        Hostname: 192.168.86.30
        Simulate: False
        ExpectOffline: True
        Inputs:
          - Name: "Sydney Dev A I1"
            Webhooks: True
          - Name: "Sydney Dev A I2"
        Outputs:
          - Name: "Sydney Dev A O1"
            Webhooks: True
          - Name: "Sydney Dev A O2"
        Meters:
          - Name: "Sydney Dev A M1"
          - Name: "Sydney Dev A M2"
      - Name: Sydney Solar
        Model: Shelly2PMG3
        Hostname: 192.168.86.35
        Simulate: True
        ExpectOffline: True
        Outputs:
          - Name: "Solar Pump Output"
            Size: "Large"
          - Name: "Solar Valve Output"
            Colour: "Blue"
        Meters:
          - Name: "Solar Pump Meter"
          - Name: "Solar Pump Output"
        TempProbes:
          - Name: "Sydney Solar"
          - Name: "Temp Roof"
          - Name: "Temp Solar Return"
          - Name: "Temp Pool Water"
      - Name: Sydney Panel EM1
        Model: ShellyEMG3
        Hostname: 192.168.86.44
        Outputs:
          - Name: "Sydney Panel EM1 O1"
        Meters:
          - Name: "Sydney Panel EM1 M1"
          - Name: "Sydney Panel EM1 M2"

docs.title: SCSmartDevice Class Reference
docs.text:
See the Getting Started Guide for a guide to getting started with the smart device package.

Unified smart-device controller.

Accepts the SCSmartDevices: config block parsed from the client app's YAML file and instantiates the appropriate hardware providers.

Signature matches the legacy ShellyControl constructor so existing client apps require minimal changes.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default logger SCLogger

SCLogger instance from sc-foundation.

required device_settings dict

The SCSmartDevices dict from the app config.

required app_wake_event Event | None

Optional threading.Event set when a webhook fires.

None

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If config is invalid or the model file cannot be loaded.

docs.title: devices property
docs.text: Normalized snapshot of all device dicts.

docs.title: inputs property
docs.text: Normalized snapshot of all input component dicts.

docs.title: meters property
docs.text: Normalized snapshot of all meter component dicts.

docs.title: outputs property
docs.text: Normalized snapshot of all output component dicts.

docs.title: temp_probes property
docs.text: Normalized snapshot of all temperature probe dicts.

docs.title: change_output(output_identity, new_state)
docs.text:
Change an output relay to the requested state.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default output_identity dict | int | str

Output dict, ID, or name.

required new_state bool

True to turn on, False to turn off.

required

Returns:

Type Description bool

(success, did_change) — success is False when the device is

bool

offline; did_change is False when the output was already in the

tuple[bool, bool]

requested state.

docs.title: does_device_have_webhooks(device)
docs.text: Return True if any component of the device has webhooks enabled.

docs.title: get_device(device_identity)
docs.text:
Return the internal device dict for the given ID, name, or dict.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default device_identity dict | int | str

Device dict, integer ID, or name string. A component dict is also accepted and returns the parent device.

required

docs.title: get_device_component(component_type, component_identity, use_index=None)
docs.text:
Return the internal component dict for the given type and identity.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default component_type str

One of "input", "output", "meter", or "temp_probe".

required component_identity int | str

Component ID (int) or name (str).

required use_index bool | None

If True, match by ComponentIndex instead of ID/name.

None

docs.title: get_device_information(device_identity, refresh_status=False)
docs.text:
Return a consolidated copy of one device and all its components.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default device_identity dict | int | str

Device dict, ID, or name.

required refresh_status bool

If True, fetch fresh state from hardware first.

False

Returns:

Type Description dict

Device dict augmented with Inputs, Outputs, Meters,

dict

and TempProbes sub-lists.

docs.title: get_device_location(device_identity)
docs.text:
Return timezone/lat/lon for a Shelly device, or None.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default device_identity dict | int | str

Device dict, ID, or name.

required

docs.title: get_device_status(device_identity)
docs.text:
Refresh the status of one device from the hardware.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default device_identity dict | int | str

Device dict, ID, or name.

required

Returns:

Type Description bool

True if the device is online and status was retrieved.

docs.title: get_input(input_identity)
docs.text:
Shorthand for get_device_component("input", input_identity).

Returns:

Type Description dict

The internal input component dict.

docs.title: get_meter(meter_identity)
docs.text:
Shorthand for get_device_component("meter", meter_identity).

Returns:

Type Description dict

The internal meter component dict.

docs.title: get_output(output_identity)
docs.text:
Shorthand for get_device_component("output", output_identity).

Returns:

Type Description dict

The internal output component dict.

docs.title: get_temp_probe(probe_identity)
docs.text:
Shorthand for get_device_component("temp_probe", probe_identity).

Returns:

Type Description dict

The internal temperature probe component dict.

docs.title: get_view()
docs.text:
Return a frozen, indexed, thread-safe snapshot of current device state.

Use this in multi-threaded applications (e.g. alongside SmartDeviceWorker) to safely read device state without holding a lock.

Returns:

Type Description SmartDeviceView

SmartDeviceView built from the current normalized status.

docs.title: initialize_settings(device_settings, refresh_status=True)
docs.text:
Reload device configuration without restarting the webhook server.

Useful for hot-reloading when the YAML config file changes at runtime.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default device_settings dict

Updated SCSmartDevices dict.

required refresh_status bool

If True (default) refresh device state after loading.

True

docs.title: install_webhook(event, component, url=None, additional_payload=None)
docs.text:
Install a webhook on a device component.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default event str

Shelly event name, e.g. "input.toggle_on".

required component dict

Component dict (from :meth:get_device_component).

required url str | None

Override the callback URL. Auto-constructed if None.

None additional_payload dict | None

Extra query-string parameters to include.

None

docs.title: is_device_online(device_identity=None)
docs.text:
Ping one device (or all devices) and return online status.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default device_identity dict | int | str | None

Device to check, or None to check all.

None

Returns:

Type Description bool

True if the selected device (or all devices) is online.

docs.title: print_device_status(device_identity=None)
docs.text: Return a human-readable status string for one device or all devices.

docs.title: print_model_library(mode_str='brief', model_id=None)
docs.text:
Return a listing of known Shelly device models.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default mode_str str

"brief" (default) or "detailed".

'brief' model_id str | None

If given, filter to one model.

None

docs.title: pull_webhook_event()
docs.text:
Return the oldest queued webhook event and remove it from the queue.

Returns:

Type Description dict | None

Event dict with keys timestamp, Event, Device,

dict | None

Component, etc.; or None if the queue is empty.

docs.title: refresh()
docs.text: Alias for :meth:refresh_all_device_statuses.

docs.title: refresh_all_device_statuses()
docs.text: Refresh status for every configured device.

docs.title: shutdown()
docs.text:
Stop the webhook server and release all resources.

Call this when the client application is terminating.

docs.title: SmartDeviceView Class Reference
docs.text:
See the Getting Started Guide for a guide to getting started with the smart device package.

Read-only facade over a SmartDeviceStatus snapshot.

Provides efficient ID-based lookups for all component types. Build a SmartDeviceView from a SmartDeviceStatus via SCSmartDevice.get_view().
